info.title: Third Party API
info.description: Deriv API provides programmatic access to trading services, account management, and market data across multiple trading platforms including CFDs, options, and peer-to-peer exchange. This REST API enables developers to integrate Deriv's trading infrastructure into their applications with comprehensive endpoints for account operations, trading activities, and real-time market information.

tags.name: Options Trading
tags.description: Options trading API: account management, OTP issuance for WebSocket auth, and WebSocket endpoints for trading and public market data.

tags.name: Options Trading - Legacy
tags.description: Read-only access to a user's pre-upgrade options trading data (statement, accounts, upgrade status). These endpoints expose data sourced from the legacy platform and will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

tags.name: Reporting
tags.description: Aggregated business reporting endpoints (e.g. per-application markup statistics).

tags.name: System
tags.description: Operational endpoints for service health and infrastructure checks.

tags.name: Payment Agent
tags.description: Endpoints for discovering payment agent profiles and executing payment-agent-facilitated deposits and withdrawals.

get.description: Returns every options trading account the authenticated user owns, including both real and demo accounts. Use this to discover the account IDs needed for placing trades, opening WebSocket connections, or resetting demo balances.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

post.description: Opens a new options trading account for the authenticated user. The account type (real or demo) and currency are taken from the request body. The newly created account is returned in the response.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

200.description: OK - returned when the account already exists (if the same parameters are provided)

201.description: Created - returned when a new account is created

post.description: Resets the balance of the specified demo account back to its default starting amount. Only demo accounts owned by the authenticated user can be reset; calling this on a real account will be rejected.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Options Trading account ID

post.summary: Issue a one-time password for WebSocket authentication
post.description: Issues a short-lived one-time password (OTP) that authenticates a subsequent WebSocket connection for the specified account. The response includes the OTP and a ready-to-use WebSocket URL with the OTP attached as a query parameter. The OTP is valid for 120 seconds and can be used only once — open the WebSocket connection promptly after calling this endpoint.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Options Trading account ID that OTP is requested for

post.summary: Buy a contract across multiple real accounts
post.description: Buys the same options contract for multiple real-money accounts in a single call. Authentication is by your application's `Deriv-App-ID` header together with the per-account Personal Access Tokens (PATs) supplied in the request body; no OAuth access token is required for this endpoint. Each entry in the request pairs an end-user PAT — which must carry the `trade` scope and own the paired account — with the real account to buy for, and the same contract parameters are applied to every account. Up to 100 entries are accepted per request. An entry whose token is invalid, or whose token does not own the paired account, is reported individually in the response without affecting the others; if no entry is valid the request is rejected with 400. This endpoint is for real-money accounts only — demo accounts are reported as per-account failures.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

contract_parameters.description: Options contract parameters applied to every account in the request (e.g. contract_type, symbol, amount, duration).

accounts.description: List of end-user token / account pairs to buy the contract for. Duplicate pairs are honoured and result in repeated purchases.

token.description: End-user Personal Access Token (PAT) authorizing the purchase on the paired account.

account_id.description: Options trading account the contract should be bought for. Must belong to the token's owner.

200.description: The request was processed. The transactions array contains one entry per account with either the purchased contract details or a per-account error.

transactions.description: Per-account purchase results. Each entry reports success (contract/transaction details) or an error for that account.

400.description: Invalid request body, or no token/account pair passed validation (each failing account is reported in errors[].field).

502.description: An upstream service was temporarily unavailable. Retry the request.

post.summary: Buy a contract across multiple demo accounts
post.description: Buys the same options contract for multiple demo accounts in a single call. Authentication is by your application's `Deriv-App-ID` header together with the per-account Personal Access Tokens (PATs) supplied in the request body; no OAuth access token is required for this endpoint. Each entry in the request pairs an end-user PAT — which must carry the `trade` scope and own the paired account — with the demo account to buy for, and the same contract parameters are applied to every account. Up to 100 entries are accepted per request. An entry whose token is invalid, or whose token does not own the paired account, is reported individually in the response without affecting the others; if no entry is valid the request is rejected with 400. This endpoint is for demo accounts only — real-money accounts are reported as per-account failures.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

contract_parameters.description: Options contract parameters applied to every account in the request (e.g. contract_type, symbol, amount, duration).

accounts.description: List of end-user token / account pairs to buy the contract for. Duplicate pairs are honoured and result in repeated purchases.

token.description: End-user Personal Access Token (PAT) authorizing the purchase on the paired account.

account_id.description: Options trading account the contract should be bought for. Must belong to the token's owner.

200.description: The request was processed. The transactions array contains one entry per account with either the purchased contract details or a per-account error.

transactions.description: Per-account purchase results. Each entry reports success (contract/transaction details) or an error for that account.

400.description: Invalid request body, or no token/account pair passed validation (each failing account is reported in errors[].field).

502.description: An upstream service was temporarily unavailable. Retry the request.

get.summary: Open a WebSocket connection for a demo account
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection scoped to a demo options trading account. Authentication requires a valid OTP obtained from the OTP endpoint, passed as the `otp` query parameter. Use this channel to subscribe to real-time market data, send trading instructions, and receive order and position updates for the demo account.

parameters.name: otp
parameters.description: One time password to authorize websocket connection. Token should be generated by endpoint /trading/v1/options/accounts/{accountId}/otp

get.summary: Open a WebSocket connection for a real account
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection scoped to a real-money options trading account. Authentication requires a valid OTP obtained from the OTP endpoint, passed as the `otp` query parameter. Use this channel to subscribe to real-time market data, send trading instructions, and receive order and position updates. Trades placed over this channel settle against the account's real balance.

parameters.name: otp
parameters.description: One time password to authorize websocket connection. Token should be generated by endpoint /trading/v1/options/accounts/{accountId}/otp

get.summary: Open a public WebSocket connection (no authentication)
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection for public options trading data. No authentication or OTP is required. Use this channel to stream real-time market data — for example quotes, ticks, and contract metadata — without needing a user account. Account-scoped actions such as placing trades or reading balances are not available on this channel.

get.summary: Get the legacy transaction statement for a loginid
get.description: Returns the historical transaction statement from the legacy options platform for the authenticated user, scoped to the default account of the loginid supplied in the `loginid` query parameter. Results can be filtered by transaction type and by a date range, expressed as a half-open interval `[date_from, date_to)` using epoch seconds. Pagination uses `limit` (default 100, maximum 999) and `offset`. Transactions are returned newest first, with the most recent `transaction_time` at the top and ties broken by `id` in descending order. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: loginid
parameters.description: Legacy loginid whose default-account statement to return. Must match `^[A-Z]+[0-9]+$`.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound as epoch seconds. A row is included iff its true `transaction_time` is `>= date_from`. Defaults to 0 (all history). Values above 2147483647 (the year-2038 epoch limit) are rejected with HTTP 422.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound as epoch seconds. A row is included iff its true `transaction_time` is `< date_to`. Defaults to current time. Values above 2147483647 (the year-2038 epoch limit) are rejected with HTTP 422.

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: Filter by transaction type. Defaults to all types.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return. Defaults to 100, maximum 999.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of transactions to skip before returning results. Use with `limit` for pagination.

404.description: User or default account not found

409.description: User migration is pending or has failed

get.summary: List the authenticated user's legacy accounts
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's accounts on the legacy options platform, grouped by loginid. Each account includes its `account_id` and `currency`. Loginids that have no accounts are omitted. When the user has no resolvable legacy accounts, the response is `{"loginids": {}}`. Use the returned loginids with the legacy statement endpoint to fetch historical transactions. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

404.description: User has no legacy account mapping

409.description: User migration is pending or has failed

get.summary: Get the user's platform upgrade status
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's current platform upgrade status. The response always has HTTP 200 and the shape `{"status": <state>}`, where `<state>` is one of `complete`, `pending`, `failed`, or `not_applicable`. These states are normal answers, not error conditions — for example, `pending` means the upgrade is still in progress, and `not_applicable` means the user never had a legacy account in the first place. Responses are returned with `Cache-Control: no-store`, so polling clients always see fresh state. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

Cache-Control.description: Always set to `no-store` so polling clients are not served stale state.

get.description: Returns aggregated markup statistics over a date range, broken down per application registered by the authenticated user. Use this to track how much markup revenue each of your registered applications has generated. The date range is specified via query parameters.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Interval start date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive, UTC.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Interval end date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive, UTC.

get.description: Returns HTTP 200 when the gateway is up and able to serve traffic. Intended for use by load balancers, uptime monitors, and infrastructure probes. The endpoint requires no authentication and returns no body payload of interest — the HTTP status alone is the signal.

Returns a paginated list of active payment agents that support the requested `currency`. Results include publicly listed agents, plus any agent you've previously received a transfer from even if that agent isn't publicly listed. An optional `country` filter further restricts to agents that serve that country. Each result includes the agent's profile and its commission rates for the requested currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Only agents that support this currency are returned.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code in lowercase (e.g. `id`). When supplied, only agents whose `countries` list includes this value are returned.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 25.

200.description: OK — paginated list of payment agents matching the supplied filters.

data.description: Payment agent list items sorted by agent ID ascending.

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid query parameters (e.g. `currency` not supplied). `errors[0].code` will be `InputError`.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope (`payment` scope required).

504.description: Gateway timeout — the service did not respond in time.

get.summary: Get a payment agent by ID
get.description: Returns the full profile of a single payment agent, including agents not publicly listed in the directory. Use this when you already have an agent's `id` and need their full details. The response always includes a `currencies` array with the agent's commission rates and withdrawal limits for each supported currency, and a `urls` array with the agent's associated websites. Pass `me` as the `id` to return the authenticated client's own agent profile.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Numeric ID of the payment agent (as returned by `GET /payment-agents/v1/agents`) or the literal string `"me"` to return the authenticated client's own agent profile. Any other non-numeric value returns `InvalidAgentID`.

400.description:
Bad request — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `InvalidAgentID` — the `id` path parameter is not a valid numeric ID or `me`

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope.

404.description: Not found — `AgentNotFound`: no payment agent exists with the given `id`.

get.description: Returns the aggregate set of countries and currencies covered by at least one active, publicly listed payment agent. Use this to determine which `currency` and `country` filter values are meaningful before calling the agent list — results may lag newly onboarded agents by a short period.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope.

post.summary: Transfer funds to a client (payment agent deposit)
post.description:
Lets an authenticated payment agent deposit funds directly into a client's Deriv wallet, identified by their account nickname (`to_nickname`). On success, returns a transaction status with `status: "complete"` and a `transaction_id`.

Business rules enforced (each maps to a specific error code if not met):

- Caller must be a registered, active payment agent (`AgentIDInvalid`, `AgentInactive`).
- Agent must support the requested `currency` (`AgentCurrencyUnsupported`).
- The recipient must not be the calling payment agent's own Deriv account (`AgentSelfTransfer`).
- `to_nickname` must match exactly one existing Deriv account (`NicknameNotFound`).
- Recipient account must be able to receive the funds (`NoClientWallet`).
- Recipient's country of residence must be served by the agent (`ClientCountryUnsupported`).
- Agent's wallet must hold enough funds to cover the transfer (`WalletFundsInsufficient`).

to_nickname.description: Deriv account nickname of the client to receive the funds.

amount.description: Amount to transfer expressed as a decimal string.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer. Must be supported by the authenticated payment agent.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes for this transaction.

request_id.description: Optional idempotency key, 1-128 characters (letters, digits, underscore, hyphen). Reusing a previous `request_id` returns `RequestIDUsed` instead of creating a duplicate transfer. Unlike withdrawals, a transfer's `request_id` cannot be used to look up status afterwards — transfers complete synchronously in this same response.

200.description: OK — the transfer was completed. `data.status` will be `"complete"` and `data.transaction_id` identifies the resulting transaction.

400.description:
Bad request — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `AgentIDInvalid` — the authenticated client is not a registered payment agent
- `AgentInactive` — the authenticated payment agent account is currently inactive
- `AgentSelfTransfer` — the `to_nickname` provided belongs to the calling payment agent. An agent cannot transfer funds to their own account
- `AgentCurrencyUnsupported` — the agent does not support the requested `currency`
- `NicknameNotFound` — `to_nickname` does not match any Deriv account
- `NoClientWallet` — the recipient account cannot receive the funds
- `ClientCountryUnsupported` — the recipient's country of residence is not covered by this agent
- `WalletFundsInsufficient` — the agent's wallet does not hold enough funds for this transfer
- `InvalidRequestIDFormat` — `request_id` does not match the required format (1-128 characters: letters, digits, underscore, hyphen)
- `RequestIDUsed` — the `request_id` has already been used. It must be unique per transfer

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope (`payment` scope required).

500.description:
Internal server error — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `NicknameLookupFailed` — the recipient account could not be resolved from `to_nickname`
- `WalletLookupFailed` — the recipient's wallet details could not be retrieved
- `TransferFailed` — the transfer could not be completed
- `InternalError` — an unexpected error occurred

These errors are transient. Retrying the request is reasonable.

post.description:
Requests a one-time verification code be sent to the client's registered contact (email or phone). Call this before submitting a withdrawal via `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw` — the code must be included as `verification_code` in that request. The code is valid until `expires_at` (epoch seconds). A new code cannot be requested before `next_request_at` (epoch seconds).

The same agent eligibility rules checked by the withdrawal endpoint are enforced here (`AgentIDInvalid`, `AgentInactive`, `AgentSelfWithdraw`, `AgentCurrencyUnsupported`), so if this request succeeds, the subsequent withdrawal will not fail those checks.

data.description: Verification code request parameters. Must match the intended withdrawal.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier of the payment agent to withdraw through.

amount.description: Intended withdrawal amount as a decimal string.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the intended withdrawal.

200.description: OK — the verification code has been sent to the client's registered contact.

data.message: Please check your email or phone for the verification code.

400.description:
Bad request — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `AgentIDInvalid` — no payment agent exists with the given `agent_id`
- `AgentInactive` — the target payment agent is not currently active
- `AgentSelfWithdraw` — the authenticated client is the registered owner of the selected payment agent. A client cannot withdraw through a payment agent account they own
- `AgentCurrencyUnsupported` — the agent does not support the requested `currency`
- `OtpRateLimitExceeded` — too many verification code requests in a short period. `errors[0].detail.next_request_at` indicates when a new request can be made

detail.message: Too many OTP requests. Please try again later.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope.

500.description:
Internal server error — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `VerificationCodeRequestFailed` — the verification code could not be dispatched
- `InternalError` — an unexpected error occurred

post.summary: Withdraw funds via a payment agent
post.description:
Lets an authenticated client withdraw funds from their Deriv wallet via a payment agent. The agent is identified by `agent_id`. The withdrawal moves the requested amount out of the client's wallet and into the agent's wallet. The agent then settles the equivalent value with the client outside of Deriv.

Before calling this endpoint, request a one-time verification code via `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw/verification_code` and supply it as `verification_code`. The code must be 6 digits and within its validity window.

Business rules enforced (each maps to a specific error code if not met):

- A payment agent with the given `agent_id` must exist and be active (`AgentIDInvalid`, `AgentInactive`).
- The authenticated client must not be the owner of the selected payment agent (`AgentSelfWithdraw`).
- The agent must support the requested `currency` (`AgentCurrencyUnsupported`).
- The client's account must hold a wallet to withdraw from (`NoClientWallet`).
- The client's country of residence must be one the agent serves (`ClientCountryUnsupported`).
- `amount` must satisfy the agent's withdrawal limits for the currency, if configured (`WithdrawalAmountMinimum`, `WithdrawalAmountMaximum`).
- The client's wallet must hold sufficient funds (`WalletFundsInsufficient`).
- `verification_code` must be the correct 6-digit code received from `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw/verification_code` (`InvalidOTP`, `OtpValidationRateLimitExceeded`).

agent_id.description: Unique identifier of the payment agent to withdraw through, as returned by `GET /payment-agents/v1/agents`.

amount.description: Amount to withdraw as a decimal string. Must satisfy the agent's `withdrawal_minimum` and `withdrawal_maximum` limits for the currency.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the withdrawal. Must be supported by the target payment agent.

request_id.description: Optional, 1-128 characters (letters, digits, underscore, hyphen). If provided, the withdrawal status can be retrieved via `GET /payment-agents/v1/withdraw/{request_id}`. Without it, status tracking is unavailable for this withdrawal. Must be unique — reusing a previous `request_id` returns `RequestIDUsed`.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes for this transaction.

200.description: OK — the withdrawal was accepted. `data.status` is `"pending"` while the transaction processes. `data.transaction_id` identifies the transaction. Use `GET /payment-agents/v1/withdraw/{request_id}` to poll for the final status if you provided a `request_id`.

400.description:
Bad request — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `AgentIDInvalid` — no payment agent exists with the given `agent_id`
- `AgentInactive` — the target payment agent is currently inactive
- `AgentSelfWithdraw` — the authenticated client is the registered owner of the selected payment agent. A client cannot withdraw through a payment agent account they own
- `AgentCurrencyUnsupported` — the agent does not support the requested `currency`
- `VerificationCodeFormatInvalid` — `verification_code` is not a 6-digit numeric string
- `NoClientWallet` — the authenticated client's account has no wallet to withdraw from
- `ClientCountryUnsupported` — the client's country of residence is not covered by this agent
- `WithdrawalAmountMinimum` — `amount` is below the agent's minimum withdrawal limit for this currency
- `WithdrawalAmountMaximum` — `amount` exceeds the agent's maximum withdrawal limit for this currency
- `WalletFundsInsufficient` — the client's wallet does not hold enough funds for this withdrawal
- `InvalidRequestIDFormat` — `request_id` does not match the required format (1-128 characters: letters, digits, underscore, hyphen)
- `RequestIDUsed` — the `request_id` has already been used. It must be unique per withdrawal
- `InvalidOTP` — the `verification_code` is incorrect. `errors[0].detail.message` contains the error message
- `OtpValidationRateLimitExceeded` — too many incorrect verification code attempts. `errors[0].detail.message` contains the error message

detail.message: Too many failed OTP validation attempts. Please try after some time.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope (`payment` scope required).

500.description:
Internal server error — `errors[0].code` will be one of:

- `ClientIdentityLookupFailed` — the authenticated client's account details could not be retrieved
- `WalletLookupFailed` — the client's wallet details could not be retrieved
- `WithdrawalFailed` — the withdrawal could not be completed
- `OTPValidationFailed` — verification code validation failed unexpectedly
- `InternalError` — an unexpected error occurred

These errors are transient. Retrying the request is reasonable.

get.summary: Get the status of a withdrawal
get.description: Returns the current status of a withdrawal previously initiated via `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw` with a `request_id`. Both the client who initiated the withdrawal and the payment agent can query this endpoint. The status progresses from `"pending"` to `"complete"`, `"rejected"`, or `"failed"`.

parameters.name: request_id
parameters.description: The `request_id` previously passed to `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw` (1-128 characters: letters, digits, underscore, hyphen). A value that doesn't match any of your withdrawals returns `RequestIDNotFound`, whether or not it matches this format.

400.description: Bad request — `RequestIDNotFound`: no withdrawal with this `request_id` is accessible to the authenticated user.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — valid token but insufficient scope.

scopes.account_manage: Write access for account creation and management
scopes.application_read: Read-only access to your registered applications
scopes.payment: Access to payment agent deposit and withdrawal operations

AppMarkupBreakdownRow.description: Per-application aggregates included in the statistics window.

app_markup_usd.description: Sum of application markup attributed to this app in USD over the requested interval.

volume_usd.description: Sum of contract volume (buy price in USD terms) for this app over the requested interval.

payout_usd.description: Sum of all potential payouts in USD terms for this app over the requested interval.

contract_count.description: Number of contracts included in the aggregates for this app.

client_count.description: Number of distinct client accounts that traded under this app in the interval.

AppMarkupStatistics.description: Markup and trading aggregates for the caller's applications over the requested date range. Root fields sum across all included apps; `breakdown` lists the same metrics per application.

total_app_markup_usd.description: Sum of application markup attributed to all included apps in the interval.

total_volume_usd.description: Sum of contract volume (buy price in USD terms) across all included apps in the interval.

total_payout_usd.description: Sum of all potential payouts (in USD terms) across all included apps in the interval.

total_contract_count.description: Total number of contracts counted toward the aggregates across all included apps.

total_client_count.description: Number of distinct client accounts that traded under any included app in the interval.

breakdown.description: Per-application rows; each entry corresponds to one `app_id` with metrics for that app only.

LegacyAccountItem.description: A single account from the legacy platform.

LegacyAccountsResponse.description: Legacy accounts grouped by loginid. Loginids with no resolvable accounts are omitted.

loginids.description: Map of loginid (e.g. CR0001) to its accounts.

LegacyMigrationStatusResponse.description: Current state of the user's platform upgrade. All known states (including pending, failed, and not_applicable) are returned with HTTP 200.

LegacyTransactionItem.description: A single historical transaction from the legacy platform for the requested loginid's default account.

account_id.description: Legacy account identifier (the default account of the requested loginid).

loginid.description: Legacy client loginid as requested (e.g. CR12345).

amount.description: Signed transaction amount as a decimal string.

balance_after.description: Account balance after the transaction as a decimal string.

transaction_time.description: Transaction timestamp as epoch seconds, computed as the floor of the underlying high-precision database timestamp. Supplying this value as `date_from` on a subsequent request always re-includes the originating row.

contract_id.description: Financial market bet ID; null for non-trading transactions.

currency.description: Account currency code (the default account's currency).

shortcode.description: Legacy contract shortcode; null for non-trading transactions.

bet_class.description: Bet class (e.g. higher_lower_bet, digit_bet, multiplier); null for non-trading transactions.

bet_type.description: Bet type (e.g. CALL, PUT, DIGITOVER); null for non-trading transactions.

symbol.description: Underlying market symbol (e.g. R_100, frxEURUSD); null for non-trading transactions.

app_id.description: Identifier of the third-party application that originated the transaction; null if not recorded.

LegacyStatementResponse.description: Statement of historical transactions from the legacy platform for the requested loginid's default account, ordered by `transaction_time` descending (ties broken by `id` descending).

count.description: Number of items in `transactions` (equals `transactions.length`). Not a total across pages.

timing.description: Time for the API to serve the request in milliseconds.

status.description: HTTP-equivalent status code for this error entry.

code.description: One of the predefined error codes.

message.description: Human-readable description of the error. Intended for logs and developer-facing surfaces; not guaranteed stable across versions.

field.description: Request element that caused the error (query param name, path param name, header name, or dot-joined body path). Omitted when not attributable to a single field.

errors.description: List of Errors from the request

PAMetadata.description: Request metadata included in every response.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request.

timing.description: Time in seconds for the service to process the request.

PAErrorDetail.description: Additional context for specific error codes. `message` is always present. `next_request_at` is included for rate-limit errors to indicate when the operation can be retried.

message.description: Human-readable description of the error condition.

next_request_at.description: Epoch timestamp (seconds) after which the operation may be retried. Present only on rate-limit errors.

PAErrorItem.description: A single error entry. Every entry carries an HTTP-equivalent `status` and a machine-readable `code`. Some errors include a `detail` object with additional context — see individual endpoint documentation.

status.description: HTTP-equivalent status code for this error.

code.description: Machine-readable error code identifying what went wrong.

PAErrorResponse.description: Standard error envelope. `data` is empty. `errors` contains one entry describing what went wrong.

data.description: Always an empty object on error.

VerificationCodeResponse.description: Confirmation that a one-time verification code has been dispatched to the client's registered contact.

message.description: Confirmation message indicating where to look for the code.
examples: Please check your email or phone for the verification code.

next_request_at.description: Epoch timestamp (seconds) before which a new verification code cannot be requested.

expires_at.description: Epoch timestamp (seconds) after which the verification code is no longer valid.

AgentCurrencyItem.description: Commission rates and withdrawal limits for a single currency supported by a payment agent.

deposit_commission.description: Commission percentage charged by the agent on deposits. Null if no deposit commission applies.

withdrawal_commission.description: Commission percentage charged by the agent on withdrawals. Null if no withdrawal commission applies.

withdrawal_minimum.description: Minimum withdrawal amount as a decimal string. Null if no minimum is enforced.

withdrawal_maximum.description: Maximum withdrawal amount as a decimal string. Null if no maximum is enforced.

PaymentAgentListItem.description: Payment agent summary as returned by the list endpoint. Commission fields reflect the rates for the `currency` used in the query.

id.description: Unique identifier of the payment agent. Pass this as `agent_id` to `POST /payment-agents/v1/withdraw`.

created_at.description: Epoch timestamp (milliseconds) when the agent was registered.

is_listed.description: Whether the agent appears in the public agent directory. `false` indicates this agent was included because you've previously received a transfer from them, not because they're publicly listed.

name.description: Display name of the payment agent.

information.description: Free-text description of the agent's services and operating details.

email.description: Contact email address of the payment agent.

payment_methods.description: Payment methods accepted by this agent (e.g. `"bank_transfer"`, `"mobile_money"`).

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes in lowercase in which this agent operates.

deposit_commission.description: Commission rate for deposits in the queried currency.

withdrawal_commission.description: Commission rate for withdrawals in the queried currency.

withdrawal_minimum.description: Minimum withdrawal amount as a decimal string for the queried currency.

withdrawal_maximum.description: Maximum withdrawal amount as a decimal string for the queried currency.

PaymentAgentDetail.description: Full payment agent profile returned by `GET /payment-agents/v1/agents/{id}`. Always includes a `currencies` array (one entry per supported currency with commission and limit settings) and a `urls` array. Unlike the list endpoint, this may include agents not publicly listed in the directory.

id.description: Unique identifier of the payment agent.

cashier_withdraw.description: Whether the agent is allowed to withdraw from the payment agent wallet (only returned when viewing own profile).

created_at.description: Epoch timestamp (milliseconds) when the agent was registered.

is_listed.description: Whether the agent appears in the public agent directory.

name.description: Display name of the payment agent.

information.description: Free-text description of the agent's services and operating details.

payment_methods.description: Payment methods accepted by this agent.

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes in lowercase in which this agent operates.

urls.description: Website URLs associated with the agent.

currencies.description: Commission and limit settings for each currency the agent supports.

AgentStatistics.description: Lists all countries and currencies currently covered by at least one active payment agent. Use these values to discover valid options for the `currency` and `country` parameters when listing agents. Results may not immediately reflect newly added agents.

available_countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes in lowercase covered by at least one active payment agent.

available_currencies.description: Sorted, deduplicated list of ISO 4217 currency codes supported by at least one active payment agent.

TransactionStatus.description: Transaction status returned by transfer, withdrawal, and withdrawal status endpoints.

status.description: Current status of the transaction. Transactions are initially `"requested"`. Transfers complete synchronously (`"complete"`). Withdrawals start as `"pending"` and eventually move to `"complete"`, `"rejected"`, or `"failed"` if it could not be processed.

transaction_id.description: Identifier of the transaction. Will be null when status is `"requested"`. If you supplied a `request_id` on a withdrawal, use `GET /payment-agents/v1/withdraw/{request_id}` to track its status independently of `transaction_id`.
